auth.title: The Workspace
auth.intro: The editorial desk for authors and their permanent record. Sign in to continue.
auth.footer: Access is provided by the publisher.

shell.notFoundTitle: There is no page at this address
shell.notFoundBody: Nothing in the record answers to it — the address may be mistyped, or the link may predate the page. Nothing has been lost.

authors.title: The Workspace
authors.intro: Each author kept here has a permanent, versioned memory. Conversations discover ideas; what is worth keeping is preserved here — so every future draft sounds more like its author, never more like AI.
authors.emptyRoster: No authors yet. The roster begins with its first author — add one to establish their permanent memory.

hero.headline2: This is where yours takes shape.
hero.lead: The Workshop is a dedicated space for authors to develop ideas, write with focus, refine their work, and bring a manuscript to life — one chapter at a time.
hero.accessNote: Access is provided by the publisher.
hero.fromIdea: From idea to manuscript. From manuscript toward publication.

journey.heading: The life of a book
journey.intro: Not a pipeline — an editorial practice. Each stage of the Workshop preserves a different kind of work, and the book carries all of it forward.

discovery.title: Discovery
discovery.body: Before the writing, the thinking: what the book is for, who it serves, and what it must not become.

bookMemory.title: Book Memory
bookMemory.body: The book's own constitution, outline, and vocabulary — its stated intentions, kept where the work can always answer to them.

manuscript.title: The Manuscript
manuscript.body: Written one chapter at a time, each a complete unit with its own purpose, then assembled into the reader's experience.

review.title: Editorial Review
review.body: A senior editorial reading of the finished draft against the book's own constitution — what it honors, and where it has drifted.

deliberation.title: Deliberation
deliberation.body: Judgment between observation and revision: the author weighs each finding and records the decision behind it.

revision.title: Revision
revision.body: The manuscript improves through the same deliberate versions it was written in — nothing overwritten, nothing lost.

publication.title: Toward Publication
publication.body: The end the whole process is built toward: a book brought to completion and readiness, on the author's terms.

workshop.heading: One place for the life of a book
workshop.intro: A serious creative environment: calm rooms for writing and review, and a permanent record that preserves every intention, version, and decision — so a book always remembers how it became itself.

authorMemory.name: Author Memory
authorMemory.body: Who the author is and how they sound, preserved across every book they will ever write.

bookMemory.name: Book Memory
bookMemory.body: Why this particular book exists, and the boundaries it agreed to keep.

writingRoom.name: The Writing Room
writingRoom.body: Where a chapter is written, its purpose and the book's memory always in the margin.

readingCopy.name: Reading Copy
readingCopy.body: The manuscript assembled for continuous reading, exactly as a reader would meet it.

findings.name: Editorial Findings
findings.body: Observations that guide revision, each citing the book's own words — never a rewrite.

deliberation.name: Editorial Deliberation
deliberation.body: A place to reason about a finding and decide, before a single word changes.

versions.name: Version History
versions.body: Every draft numbered and kept; the permanent record only grows.

constitutionReview.name: Constitution Review
constitutionReview.body: A reading of the whole manuscript against the book's founding intent.

authorship.heading: The pen stays in your hand

authorship.title: Authorship is the foundation.
authorship.body: Automation serves the author's voice; it is never the source of it. The platform will not write a book for you, and would not be worth using if it did.

complete.title: Complete before perfect.
complete.body: Finish the thought before polishing the sentence. A whole draft can be judged and matured; a fragment can only be fussed over.

deliberate.title: Revision is deliberate.
deliberate.body: Every change is a decision, worded as one — and the reasoning is kept, so a book always remembers how it became itself.

publication.heading: Built with publication in view
publication.body: Every stage of the Workshop points toward a finished book. Publishing capabilities will grow within the ecosystem; today, the Workshop's work is making the manuscript worthy of them — developed, reviewed, revised, and preserved on the author's terms.

cta.heading: Your book has a place to take shape.
cta.body: The Workshop is where authors and their books are kept — deliberately, permanently, in the author's own voice.

footer.statement: An editorial house with a memory — a dedicated space where books are developed, refined, and kept.

study.emptyBooks: No books yet. Every book opened here begins under this author’s established memory.
study.assembledMemoryHint: the exact record future AI assistance will receive — active, finalized versions only

form.addIntro: This opens the author’s permanent record. Their four memory documents — Writing Philosophy, Author Bible, Voice Profile, and Editorial Decisions — are created with it, ready to be established.
form.slugPlaceholder: derived from the name if left blank
form.editIntro: The author’s identity, as the imprint keeps it. The record’s address remains <address>/{slug}</address> — it was set when the record was opened and does not change.

study.assembledMemoryHint: the exact record future AI assistance will receive — the author’s memory first, then the book’s; active, finalized versions only

form.addIntro: This opens the book’s record under this author, together with its three memory documents — Book Constitution, Master Outline, and Concept Dictionary — ready to be established. The record will note which of the author’s memory versions the book begins under.
form.editIntro: The book’s identity, as the imprint keeps it. The record’s address remains <address>/{slug}</address> — it was set when the record was opened and does not change. Why the book exists belongs to its Constitution, not this form.
form.title: Title
form.subtitle: Subtitle
form.slugPlaceholder: derived from the title if left blank
form.statusHint: Where the book stands in its life, from Discovery to Archived — a stated fact, not a gate.
form.languageHintNew: The language the manuscript is written in. Future editorial reviews will respond in this language. It does not change the language of the platform itself.
form.languageHintEdit: The language the manuscript is written in. Future editorial reviews will respond in this language; reviews already performed keep the language they were performed in. It does not change the language of the platform itself.

book_constitution.label: Book Constitution
book_constitution.description: Why this book exists and what it is not: premise, purpose, the promise to the reader, audience, and boundaries.

master_outline.label: Master Outline
master_outline.description: The shape the book takes — parts, chapters, and the order of its argument or story.

concept_dictionary.label: Concept Dictionary
concept_dictionary.description: What the book's words mean: named ideas, canonical definitions, and distinctions the book depends on.

errors.slugUnusable: A usable slug could not be derived; please provide one.
errors.languageInvalid: The manuscript language is not a valid language tag.
errors.bookSlugTaken: This author already has a book at “{slug}”.
errors.bookCreateFailed: The book could not be created.
errors.recordSaveFailed: The record could not be saved.
errors.recordsMigrationMissing: The database is missing the Capability 2 migration — apply supabase/migrations/20260705000000_book_records.sql (docs/setup.md §2).

overview.title: The Manuscript
overview.emptyManuscript: The manuscript begins with its first chapter. Your <link>Master Outline</link> already holds the shape — open it beside you and add the first chapter here.
overview.partsEmpty: Parts are optional. A manuscript without them reads as one sequence of chapters; add a part when the book needs larger movements.

form.addIntro: A chapter is the unit of authorship: it carries its own purpose and its own history. The words come later, in the chapter itself — this opens its place in the manuscript.
form.editIntro: The chapter’s identity, as the manuscript keeps it. Its address remains <address>/{slug}</address>; its number is always computed from its place in the manuscript.
form.title: Title
form.coreQuestionHint: the single question this chapter exists to answer
form.outlinePlaceholder: the part of the Master Outline this chapter serves

writingRoom.noBrief: No brief yet — give the chapter a purpose from its record.
writingRoom.nowAtVersion: the chapter is now at Version {number}
writingRoom.draftOpenContinueWriting: A draft is open for this chapter — <link>continue writing</link>.
writingRoom.newNextIntro: Starting from the current active version — write it into the next version. It is saved as a draft until you make it active.
writingRoom.activationNote: Activating finalizes the text permanently; discarding removes the draft. Neither touches earlier versions. Nothing is saved until you save it.
writingRoom.audioDraftNote: Reads the draft as last saved.
writingRoom.conceptsNotEstablished: The Concept Dictionary has not been established.
writingRoom.chapterContextHint: the exact record future AI assistance would receive for this chapter — active, finalized versions only

readingCopy.title: Reading Copy
readingCopy.emptyManuscript: The manuscript begins with its first chapter.

progress.inParts: in {count, plural, one {# part} other {# parts}}
progress.draftsOpen: {count, plural, one {# draft} other {# drafts}} open
progress.openFindings: {count, plural, one {# open finding} other {# open findings}}

errors.coreQuestionRequired: The chapter's Core Question is required — the single question it exists to answer.
errors.slugUnusable: A usable slug could not be derived from the title.
errors.chapterSlugTaken: This manuscript already has a chapter at “{slug}” — retitle slightly.
errors.chapterCreateFailed: The chapter could not be created.
errors.chapterSaveFailed: The chapter could not be saved.
errors.partCreateFailed: The part could not be created.
errors.chapterMoveFailed: The chapter could not be moved.
errors.contentRequired: A version needs content before it can be saved.
errors.contentRequiredToActivate: A version needs content to be activated.
errors.draftAlreadyOpen: A draft is already open for this chapter; continue writing it instead.
errors.draftCreateFailed: The draft could not be created.
errors.draftSaveFailed: The draft could not be saved.
errors.activateFailed: The version could not be activated.
errors.discardFailed: The draft could not be discarded.
errors.manuscriptMigrationMissing: The database is missing the manuscript migration — apply supabase/migrations/20260708000000_manuscript_foundation.sql (docs/setup.md §2).

documentRoom.readingSuperseded: You are reading a superseded version.
documentRoom.draftOpenContinue: A draft is open for this document — <link>continue editing it</link>.
documentRoom.emptyDraftOpen: A draft is open but nothing is active yet — <link>continue editing the draft</link>.
documentRoom.newFirstIntro: This will become Version 1, saved as a draft until you make it active.
documentRoom.newNextIntro: Starting from the current active version — edit it into the next version. It is saved as a draft until you make it active.
documentRoom.draftPrivacy: A draft is private working space. Nothing reaches the permanent record — or any future AI context — until you make it active.
documentRoom.activationNote: Activating finalizes the text permanently; discarding removes the draft. Neither touches earlier versions.
documentRoom.sourceNotePlaceholder: e.g. distilled from a voice conversation, July 2026

writing_philosophy.label: Writing Philosophy
writing_philosophy.description: What this author believes about writing itself — the principles that govern every decision below.

author_bible.label: Author Bible
author_bible.description: The permanent record of who this author is: history, themes, convictions, and worldview.

voice_profile.label: Voice Profile
voice_profile.description: How this author sounds: rhythm, vocabulary, sentence habits, and tone.

editorial_decisions.label: Editorial Decisions
editorial_decisions.description: Choices the author has committed to, recorded once so they are never re-litigated.

errors.fullNameRequired: The author's full name is required.
errors.slugUnusable: A usable slug could not be derived; please provide one.
errors.slugTaken: The slug “{slug}” is already in use.
errors.authorCreateFailed: The author could not be created.
errors.recordSaveFailed: The record could not be saved.
errors.contentRequired: A version needs content before it can be saved.
errors.contentRequiredToActivate: A version needs content to be activated.
errors.draftAlreadyOpen: A draft is already open for this document; continue editing it instead.
errors.draftCreateFailed: The draft could not be created.
errors.draftSaveFailed: The draft could not be saved.
errors.activateFailed: The version could not be activated.
errors.discardFailed: The draft could not be discarded.
errors.workflowMigrationMissing: The database is missing the Phase B workflow functions — apply supabase/migrations/20260703000000_author_memory_workflow.sql (docs/setup.md §2).
errors.bookMemoryMigrationMissing: The database is missing the Book Memory migration — apply supabase/migrations/20260706000000_book_memory_documents.sql (docs/setup.md §2).

overview.title: The imprint’s operations
overview.intro: The same account and session as your Workspace, in an operational view — for running Huerta Group Publishing rather than writing within it. This area is read-oriented for now, growing as the imprint’s operations take shape.

blurbs.authors: The author roster as the imprint holds it, independent of any one book.
blurbs.books: Every book across every author — the whole shelf in one place.
blurbs.reviewRuns: Constitution Review runs across the platform, and how each finished.
blurbs.aiUsage: Where the platform's use of external editorial models is accounted for.
blurbs.system: Configuration, migrations, and platform health — in plain terms.

overview.statusNote: Commercial plans, billing, and public access are not configured. Administration reads the platform; it does not yet change author content, and nothing here alters a manuscript, a review, or the permanent record. Those remain the author’s, in the <link>Workspace</link>.

authors.intro: Every author the platform holds. Read-only — the roster reflects the record; it never edits it.
authors.emptyNone: No authors exist on the platform yet.

authorDetail.openWorkspace: Open this author in the Workspace →
authorDetail.emptyBooks: This author has no books yet.
authorDetail.chaptersOf: {written, number}/{count, plural, one {# chapter} other {# chapters}}

books.intro: Every book across every author, with its lifecycle stage and current editorial state. Read-only.
books.emptyNone: No books exist on the platform yet.
books.chaptersWritten: {written, number}/{count, plural, one {# chapter} other {# chapters}} written

bookDetail.openBook: Open the book in the Workspace →
bookDetail.openFindings: Open the Findings in the Workspace →
bookDetail.inspectionNote: These open the author-facing Workspace for inspection — not an administrative edit mode.

reviewRuns.intro: Every review across the platform, and how each is progressing. Read-only — Administration observes the review lifecycle; it never starts, continues, or changes one.
reviewRuns.legendPending: created or actively performing a reading.
reviewRuns.legendIncomplete: intentionally paused between chunks and resumable; not a failure.
reviewRuns.legendFailed: execution ended with a failure; findings raised beforehand are preserved.
reviewRuns.emptyNone: No review runs exist on the platform yet.
reviewRuns.emptyNoMatch: No review runs match these filters.

reviewRunDetail.incompleteNote: This review paused between chunks and can be continued by the author from the Workspace. It is not a failure.
reviewRunDetail.countsScoped: Counts are scoped to this run. Earlier runs’ findings are preserved separately.
reviewRunDetail.inspectionNote: These open the author-facing Workspace for inspection — not an administrative edit mode.

aiUsage.intro: Where the platform's use of external editorial models will be accounted for — reading now, and one day listening.
aiUsage.today1: Nothing is metered here yet; model usage is not instrumented.
aiUsage.today2: Editorial review runs against configured model credentials, bounded by caps held in code.
aiUsage.deferred1: Usage and cost accounting for editorial review and audio synthesis.
aiUsage.deferred2: The commercial model for Audio Review — metering, credits, plan placement, and the line between editorial listening and audiobook production — a deliberate future decision, not this phase.
aiUsage.deferred3: No spending controls or billing here.

system.intro: The platform's configuration and health, in plain terms — how Huerta Group Publishing is wired, without changing it from here.
system.today1: Configuration lives in environment variables and database migrations, production-first.
system.today2: Row Level Security is the security boundary; the application never uses elevated database access.
system.deferred1: A read-only view of configured integrations and migration state.
system.deferred2: Health indicators drawn from real signals — never invented status.
system.deferred3: No configuration writes and no feature flags from Administration in this phase.

models.heading: Editorial review models
models.note: Read live from the OpenAI account this deployment is configured with. The key never leaves the server; only candidate identifiers for the Editorial AI Engine are listed.
models.noneFound: No candidate identifiers are available to this key.
models.unavailable: The availability check could not reach OpenAI — details are in the server log.
models.policyNote: How the Editorial AI Engine resolves a model for each reading, from trusted server configuration. Read-only; no keys are shown.

deletion.authorLede: This removes the author and every record that exists because of them — books, manuscripts, chapter versions, memory documents, review runs, findings, and deliberations. Nothing is archived; nothing can be recovered.
deletion.bookLede: This removes the book and every record that exists because of it — its manuscript, chapter versions, memory documents, review runs, findings, and deliberations. Nothing is archived; nothing can be recovered.
deletion.linkedAccountWarning: This author is linked to a user account. Deleting the record does not delete the account, but the account will lose its author record.
deletion.authorDangerNote: Deleting an author is permanent and removes every owned book and record. It is intended for test data and deliberate permanent-deletion requests — never for routine housekeeping.
deletion.bookDangerNote: Deleting a book is permanent and removes its entire manuscript, memory, and review history. It is intended for test data and deliberate permanent-deletion requests — never for routine housekeeping.
deletion.dependencyNote: Counts are read from the live database at this moment.
deletion.authorTypeToConfirm: Type the author’s full name to confirm: {name}
deletion.bookTypeToConfirm: Type the exact book title to confirm: {title}
deletion.acknowledgment: I understand this action is permanent: manuscripts, versions, reviews, findings, and history will be deleted and cannot be recovered.

messages.authorConfirmationMismatch: The typed name does not match the author’s current full name. Nothing was deleted.
messages.bookConfirmationMismatch: The typed title does not match the book’s current title. Nothing was deleted.
messages.acknowledgmentRequired: The permanence acknowledgment is required. Nothing was deleted.
messages.authorDeleteFailed: The author could not be deleted. Nothing was removed; the details are in the server log.
messages.bookDeleteFailed: The book could not be deleted. Nothing was removed; the details are in the server log.
messages.deletionNotAuthorized: Permanent deletion is restricted to staff.
messages.authorDeleted: {name} and every dependent record were permanently deleted.
messages.bookDeleted: “{title}” and every dependent record were permanently deleted.

account.title: Account
account.intro: The platform as it presents itself to you. Preferences here shape menus, labels, dates, and platform instructions — never the words of any manuscript.

language.label: Interface language
language.explanation: The language of the platform's own chrome — menus, labels, dates, and instructions. This is not the language of any manuscript: each book states its own manuscript language on its record, and editorial reviews follow the book, not this setting.
language.onlyEnglish: A language appears here only when its complete interface and editorial workflow can be trusted. Spanish is available as an internal pilot.

severityMeaning.concern: worth resolving before this stage ends

page.title: The Findings
page.intro: What review sees: observations that guide revision without touching a word. The manuscript improves through the same deliberate versions it was written in.
page.emptyFirst: Findings are what review sees: observations that guide revision without touching a word. Raise the first from a chapter's page, or from here.

run.readingNow: A {reviewType} is reading the manuscript now{progress}. This takes a few minutes; refresh to see its findings when it pauses or finishes.
run.findingsSoFar: {count, plural, one {# finding} other {# findings}} so far
run.pausedNote: This review paused before finishing. The findings it has raised are preserved below; continue to read the rest.
run.continueWait: The reviewer reads while this page waits — expect a minute or two before the Findings return.
run.didNotFinish: did not finish — findings raised before the failure are preserved
run.earlierNote: Only the most recent review’s findings are counted above. Findings from earlier readings remain below, each marked “from an earlier review.”

form.title: Raise a finding
form.intro: An observation, made permanent: what was seen, and why it matters. Findings guide revision; they never touch the text.
form.anchoredNote: — anchored to the version being read.
form.explanationHint: what was seen, and why it matters

review.title: Request a Constitution Review
review.intro: A senior editorial reading with one question: does the completed manuscript still honor the Book Constitution? The reviewer observes, identifies, and explains — it never touches a word. Its findings arrive below yours, with the same standing, and every finding must cite the Constitution’s own words.
review.noConstitution: The Book Constitution has not been established — there is nothing to review against. Establish it first; the Constitution is the law this reviewer checks.
review.disclosureOutbound: The Constitution, the Outline, and the manuscript text are sent to OpenAI for this review, and for nothing else. Identity documents are not sent.
review.disclosureCost: A review of this size typically costs a few dollars and takes a few minutes. Each run is a fresh reading — running again may see differently, and repeated runs may restate ground already covered.
review.busyReading: A review is already reading this manuscript — return to <link>the Findings</link> in a few minutes.
review.busyUnfinished: An unfinished review is waiting to continue — pick it up from <link>the Findings</link>.
review.requestWait: The reviewer reads while this page waits — expect a few minutes before the Findings return.

errors.explanationRequired: A finding needs an explanation — what was seen, and why it matters.
errors.anchorRequired: A chapter finding must anchor to the version observed.
errors.raiseFailed: The finding could not be raised.
errors.resolveFailed: The finding could not be resolved.
errors.setAsideFailed: The finding could not be set aside.
errors.reopenFailed: The finding could not be reopened.
errors.findingsMigrationMissing: The database is missing the Editorial Findings migration — apply supabase/migrations/20260711000000_editorial_findings.sql (docs/setup.md §2).
errors.reviewNotConfigured: Editorial review is not configured: OPENAI_API_KEY is not set.
errors.bookNotFound: The book could not be found.
errors.nothingToReview: There is nothing to review yet — the manuscript has no written chapters.
errors.alreadyReading: A review is already reading this manuscript. Let it finish first.
errors.unfinishedWaiting: An unfinished review is waiting to continue. Continue it from the Findings.
errors.startFailed: The review could not be started. If this persists, the reviewer's migration may not be applied.
errors.alreadyContinuing: This review is already being continued. Let it finish first.
errors.nothingToContinue: There is no unfinished review to continue.
errors.signInRequired: Sign in to request a review.
errors.reviewMigrationMissing: The database is missing a Constitution Review migration — apply supabase/migrations/20260712000000_constitution_review.sql and the chunked-execution migrations 20260714/20260715 (docs/setup.md §2).
errors.reviewDidNotFinish: The review could not finish. Findings raised before the failure are preserved below.
errors.reviewPausedTokenBudget: The review paused after reaching its configured usage limit. Everything read so far is preserved — the review is incomplete, not failed, and can be continued once the usage policy or available credit is addressed.

page.title: Deliberation
page.intro: Judgment, preserved. The finding observed; this memo decides what the book will do about it, and why. The revisions themselves remain the work of versions — a deliberation edits nothing.
page.adoptionNote: Adoption makes the judgment permanent — like activating a version. Until then, the draft is yours to shape or discard.
page.implementedNotePlaceholder: where the judgment was carried out

form.question: Question
form.judgmentHint: what the book will do — never the words that will do it
form.affectedHint: what this touches, in your own words

errors.adoptionRequires: Adoption requires a judgment and its reasoning.
errors.saveFailed: The deliberation could not be saved.
errors.adoptFailed: The judgment could not be adopted.
errors.implementFailed: The deliberation could not be marked implemented.
errors.discardFailed: The draft could not be discarded.
errors.deliberationMigrationMissing: The database is missing the Editorial Deliberation migration — apply supabase/migrations/20260713000000_editorial_deliberation.sql (docs/setup.md §2).

shared.workspaceTitle: The workspace hit an unexpected error
shared.workspaceBody: Nothing in the permanent record has been lost. Try again; if the error persists, the server logs carry the details{digest, select, none {} other { (digest {digest})}}.
shared.adminBody: Administration reads live platform data; a query may have failed. Try again; if it persists, the server logs carry the details{digest, select, none {} other { (digest {digest})}}.

common.intro: These values are the defaults for this author's books. A book may later override them. Review-affecting settings apply only to future reviews — past reviews are never changed.

account.heading: Display & comfort
account.note: How the platform itself appears to you. These preferences affect the interface only — never a manuscript's typeface, size, or measure, and never editorial review.

reducedMotion.label: Reduce motion
reducedMotion.description: Shorten or remove non-essential interface transitions. Functional loading feedback is always kept.

interfaceTextScale.label: Interface text size
interfaceTextScale.description: The size of the platform's own menus, labels, and instructions. This never changes any manuscript.

author.title: Author Settings
author.intro: Editorial and manuscript-display defaults for {name}. These become the defaults for this author's books; a book may later override them.
author.editorialNote: How future reviews of this author's books read by default. Tone changes phrasing, not truth or severity; optional observations affect only optional Notes; emphasis asks for extra attention without disabling any area. Current reviews and past findings never change.
author.displayNote: How manuscript prose is presented while reading and drafting. Display only — the stored text is never changed.

tone.label: Editorial tone
tone.description: Tone changes the phrasing of feedback — never the facts, the severity, or which issues are raised.

observations.label: Optional observations
observations.description: “Omit” hides only optional Note-level observations. Suggestions and Concerns are always included when valid.

emphasis.label: Editorial emphasis
emphasis.description: Ask for extra attention in up to two areas. Emphasis never disables any other review area.

regionalConvention.label: Regional convention
regionalConvention.description: A regional editorial style for spelling and usage. This does not change the manuscript's language.

includeAuthorMemory.label: Include Author Memory in reviews
includeAuthorMemory.description: When on, eligible future reviews consider the Author Memory. Current reviews and past findings never change.

manuscriptFont.label: Manuscript typeface
manuscriptFont.description: The typeface for reading and drafting manuscript prose. Display only — the stored text never changes.

editorTextScale.label: Editor text size
editorTextScale.description: The size of manuscript text while drafting. Display only.

writingMeasure.label: Writing measure
writingMeasure.description: The line length of manuscript prose. Display only.

errors.tooManyEmphasisAreas: Choose at most two emphasis areas.
errors.settingsSaveFailed: The settings could not be saved. Please try again.
errors.settingsNotAuthorized: You don't have access to these settings.
errors.bookSettingsSaveFailed: The settings could not be saved. Please try again.
errors.bookSettingsNotAuthorized: You don't have access to these settings.

book.title: Book Settings
book.intro: Overrides of {author}'s defaults for {book}. An unset value keeps using the author default.
book.editorialNote: How future reviews of this book read, overriding the author defaults. Tone changes phrasing, not truth or severity; optional observations affect only optional Notes; emphasis asks for extra attention without disabling any area. Current reviews and past findings never change.
book.reviewContextNote: Which optional context eligible future reviews consider for this book. The Book Constitution and Master Outline are always required and can never be disabled here; prior findings and repair context are never optional.
book.displayNote: How this book's manuscript prose is presented while reading and drafting. Display only — the stored text is never changed. A book override takes effect immediately on the manuscript surfaces.

conceptDictionary.label: Concept Dictionary in reviews
conceptDictionary.description: When included, eligible future reviews consider this book's Concept Dictionary. Display and review context only — the Book Constitution and Master Outline remain required.
